seo.title: 10 Best Hotels in Trang 2026 — City Center, Islands & Beach Resorts
seo.metaDescription: Find the best hotels in Trang for every budget. From city-center guesthouses near the train station to eco-resorts on Koh Kradan. Real prices, honest reviews, and booking tips.

intro: Trang's accommodation falls into two categories: city-center hotels and guesthouses near the train station (your base for dim sum breakfasts and day trips), and island eco-resorts on Koh Kradan, Koh Mook, and Koh Ngai (for immersive beach stays). Most visitors spend 1-2 nights in the city, then extend to an island. Budget accommodation is excellent value — Trang has none of Krabi's tourist inflation. Here are 10 hotels across all budgets — every one a real property with real prices.

hotels.name: A Local Something
hotels.description: Trang's most popular hostel occupies a thoughtfully renovated shophouse with artistic design touches, smart dorm beds with privacy curtains and lockers, and a rooftop cafe with city views. The owner is known for exceptional local advice — insider tips on dim sum spots and organizing island tours at local prices. 5 minutes walk from the train station and the main dim sum strip on Thanon Ratchadamnoen.
highlights: Rooftop cafe — great for planning morning dim sum routes
Hostel staff arrange island tours at local (non-tourist) prices
5-minute walk to Trang train station and Thanon Ratchadamnoen dim sum shophouses

hotels.name: Yamawa Guesthouse
hotels.description: A well-regarded budget guesthouse run by a friendly local family who know Trang Province inside out. Private rooms with air conditioning and hot water, clean bathrooms, and strong WiFi. The owner helps guests plan efficient itineraries combining dim sum breakfast, island day trips, and cave visits. Good value private rooms — a step up from dorm sleeping for only slightly more money.
highlights: Knowledgeable local owner — invaluable for planning island logistics
Clean private rooms with AC at hostel-adjacent prices
Walking distance to Trang's main food streets

hotels.name: Sri Trang Hotel
hotels.description: Trang's most historic hotel, founded in 1952 and occupying a prominent position directly opposite Trang train station. The building has been modernized while retaining original southern Thai character. Rooms range from basic fan rooms to air-conditioned deluxe options. The location is the main selling point — step off the overnight train and you're already at your hotel, with dim sum restaurants a 3-minute walk away.
highlights: Directly opposite Trang train station — perfect for overnight train arrivals
One of Trang's oldest operating hotels — 1952 heritage building
Walking distance to all city-center dim sum restaurants and the night market

hotels.name: Trang Hotel
hotels.description: The most established mid-range hotel in Trang City, well-located on Thanon Phra Ram VI near the commercial center. Comfortable rooms with reliable AC, hot water, and WiFi. The ground-floor coffee shop is a meeting point for Trang's business community and a good place to arrange guides and tour operators. Consistently good reviews for cleanliness, service, and restaurant quality.
highlights: Central location on Thanon Phra Ram VI — walkable to dim sum and night market
In-house restaurant serves good southern Thai and Chinese food
Reliable mid-range standard — the reference hotel for business travelers in Trang

hotels.name: Rua Rasada Hotel
hotels.description: The most popular upscale hotel in Trang City — a modern 4-star property with a large swimming pool, fitness center, and multiple restaurants. Spacious rooms with proper sound insulation, good beds, and reliable WiFi. The rooftop pool bar is excellent for evening drinks. The hotel's travel desk organizes island tours directly from the hotel. Rated 8.6/10 on Booking.com — exceptionally strong for Trang.
highlights: Large swimming pool — a rarity in Trang's hotel scene
8.6/10 Booking.com rating — the best-reviewed hotel in Trang City
In-house travel desk for island tour bookings — removes logistics stress

hotels.name: Anantara Si Kao Resort
hotels.area: Si Kao (40 km south of Trang City, near Hat Chao Mai National Park)
hotels.description: Trang Province's most luxurious hotel — an Anantara resort on the mangrove-backed coastline near Hat Chao Mai National Park. Thatched villas and rooms set in tropical gardens with an infinity pool overlooking the Andaman Sea. The resort arranges private speedboat transfers to Koh Mook and Koh Kradan, and offers kayaking through the adjacent mangroves. The isolation is total — peaceful and immersive — but you're dependent on the resort for transport.
highlights: Anantara brand luxury — one of Thailand's top boutique hotel groups
Private speedboat access to Hat Chao Mai National Park islands
Infinity pool overlooking the Andaman Sea and mangrove coastline

hotels.name: Pawapi Beach Resort
hotels.description: The best-value resort on Koh Mook, positioned on Charlie Beach — the island's main swimming beach and the departure point for kayaking to the Emerald Cave. Wooden bungalows with sea views, an in-house restaurant with fresh-caught fish, and direct beach access. Staying here means you can kayak to the Emerald Cave at dawn (before tour groups arrive) for the genuine experience. No roads, no cars — just island life.
highlights: Charlie Beach location — 20-minute kayak to Emerald Cave (kayaks for rent)
Stay overnight for dawn Emerald Cave access before tour boats arrive
Genuine Thai island experience — fishing village nearby, no tourist infrastructure

hotels.name: Koh Kradan Paradise Beach Resort
hotels.area: Koh Kradan (inside Hat Chao Mai National Park)
hotels.description: One of the few accommodation options on Koh Kradan — this eco-resort sits directly on the island's main beach inside the national park. Bungalows are simple but clean with air conditioning and beachfront terraces. The setting is extraordinary: no other accommodation visible along the beach, park rules limit generator hours, and after the day-trippers leave at 4 PM the beach belongs almost entirely to guests. The national park fee (200 THB) is charged separately on arrival.
highlights: Direct beachfront on one of Thailand's best-rated beaches
After 4 PM the beach is exclusively for overnight guests — near-private beach experience
Snorkeling reef is directly in front of the resort (bring your own gear or rent on-site)

hotels.name: Koh Ngai Thanya Resort
hotels.description: A comfortable mid-range island resort on Koh Ngai's western beach — one of Trang Province's most photogenic island settings, with a long white-sand beach facing the Trang mainland mountains. Bungalows are set among palm trees with beach or garden views. The island has several resorts unlike Koh Kradan so there's slightly more choice for dining. Ferries and speedboats connect from Trang Pier (1.5 hours).
highlights: Koh Ngai's western beach — exceptional sunset views toward the Trang mainland
More accessible than Koh Kradan — slightly shorter boat journey from Trang
Multiple resort options on Koh Ngai give more dining flexibility than single-resort islands

hotels.name: Mook Lamai Resort and Spa
hotels.description: The most upscale option on Koh Mook — a spa resort on the island's quieter southern shore with larger villas, a swimming pool, and a full-service spa (unusual for such a remote island). Private boat transfer from Trang Pier (1.5 hours) is included in the rate. The spa uses local herbs and southern Thai massage techniques. The Emerald Cave is a 15-minute boat ride from the resort dock.
highlights: Swimming pool and full spa — luxurious amenities on an island with minimal infrastructure
Private boat transfer from Trang Pier included in rate
15-minute resort boat to Emerald Cave — most exclusive access available on Koh Mook

bookingTips: Book island accommodation (Koh Kradan, Koh Mook, Koh Ngai) well in advance for December-January — room counts are tiny and peak season books out weeks ahead
City-center hotels near Trang train station are rarely full — you can usually book 1-2 days ahead in shoulder and low season
Island resorts typically include boat transfer from Trang Pier in their quoted rates — confirm this before booking to avoid surprise transport costs
Low season (May-October) island resorts may close completely or run at skeleton service — always confirm opening before booking
Rua Rasada Hotel is the best city-center hotel for price versus quality — book directly for the best rates and ask about island tour packages
For Koh Kradan, staying overnight is significantly better than day-tripping — you get the beach to yourself from 4 PM to 9 AM when day boats are absent
